text.value:
The Azure REST API specifications repository contains numerous README files, each associated with various parts of the Azure REST API. These README files typically provide information about specific services, how to use APIs, code samples, and guidelines for developers. Here are a few examples of README files and their contexts:

1. [eng/tools/lint-diff/README.md](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/ad2d848416d5b898870a6855790e92057c0f2d2d/eng/tools/lint-diff/README.md): This file likely explains how to use lint tools to compare differences in code or specifications, ensuring consistency and quality in API definitions.

2. [specification/app/data-plane/readme.md](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/ad2d848416d5b898870a6855790e92057c0f2d2d/specification/app/data-plane/readme.md): This README might detail the data plane APIs related to Azure's application services, including documentation on how to interact with those APIs.

3. [specification/nginx/resource-manager/readme.md](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/ad2d848416d5b898870a6855790e92057c0f2d2d/specification/nginx/resource-manager/readme.md): This file possibly provides instructions and information relating to the resource management of NGINX services within Azure.

4. [specification/cognitiveservices/data-plane/AnomalyDetector/readme.md](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/ad2d848416d5b898870a6855790e92057c0f2d2d/specification/cognitiveservices/data-plane/AnomalyDetector/readme.md): This README might discuss the usage and capabilities of the Anomaly Detector APIs in Azure's Cognitive Services.

Each README file serves as a guide to understand and implement the related API services provided by Azure, including setup, authentication, sample requests, responses, and other critical aspects necessary for developers to integrate their applications with Azure services.
Please summarize the Azure REST API specifications Readme
